detail: Selected accession 0001571049-15-000695 (DEFM14A) as the anchor filing because it provided the strongest chronology section under deterministic scoring.
Merged the full legal name "Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz" with the filing shorthand "Wachtell Lipton" into a single advisor actor.
Classified Longview as activist rather than bidder because the chronology describes it as a stockholder advocating for a sale and a possible rollover participant, not an acquirer submitting a bid.
Classified JANA Partners as activist rather than bidder because the filing describes JANA as a 13D stockholder pressing for strategic alternatives and a sale, not as an acquisition counterparty.

Classified Industry Participant as a strategic bidder because the board evaluated a merger or acquisition with a privately held industry peer and later considered whether to invite that party into the sale process.

Classified Buyer Group as a financial bidder group because the named late-stage bidders arise from the cohort of 15 potentially interested financial buyers described on line 1336, and the filing later refers to Buyer Group as a group with multiple members.

Classified Bidder 2 as a financial bidder because the identified late-stage bidders are drawn from the 15 potentially interested financial buyers that signed confidentiality and standstill agreements.

Classified Bidder 3 as a grouped financial bidder because the filing expressly states that two bidders were authorized to work together, and the late-stage bidder cohort comes from the 15 financial buyers under NDA.

Applied bidder/financial classification to unnamed_1 through unnamed_12 because line 1336 describes 15 potentially interested financial buyers under confidentiality and standstill agreements, while only three later bidder labels are explicitly named in the bookmarked chronology.

Recorded "approximately 15 parties had expressed interest" as expected_count = 15 because count_assertions.json requires an integer while preserving the filing's approximate wording in assertion_text and source_text.

Minted 12 unnamed financial bidder placeholders from the 15-buyer NDA cohort after separately tracking Buyer Group, Bidder 2, and Bidder 3; this is a usable group-level scaffold rather than a fully reconciled underlying-party census because Bidder 3 later represents two bidders working together.

Preserved both the six indications-of-interest count and the five bidder-group count as separate filing assertions rather than forcing reconciliation, because the chronology later collapses two bidders into the grouped label "Bidder 3."

Recorded the August 19, 2014 public announcement as sale_press_release rather than target_sale_public because the filing specifically describes the disclosure as the Company issuing a press release and does not describe a separate non-press public sale-process announcement.
Filing says Bidder 2 increased its indication from October 30 to November 2, 2014; recorded as 2014-11-01 with date_precision=approximate.

Did not force the six October 30 indications of interest into six actor-linked proposal rows because the filing discloses explicit pricing only for Buyer Group, Bidder 2, and one later Bidder 3 component, while the remaining first-round bidders are aggregated or unnamed in the allowed source set.

Recorded Bidder 3's December 10, 2014 communication as drop_below_m rather than proposal because the filing says Bidder 3 stated only that its valuation would not be above the current stock price and therefore did not submit a written offer.

Used supplementary filing 0001193125-15-087589 (8-K) to source deal.json date_effective and completed outcome because the primary Background of the Merger chronology ends at execution on December 14, 2014.
